title: Glechoma fordii
taxonomy: Glechoma fordii is classified within the plant family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family. This classification is based on its shared characteristics with other species within this family. The Lamiaceae family comprises approximately 7,000 species of flowering plants, many of which are aromatic and herbaceous. Glechoma fordii is a type of perennial or annual herb, and its classification within Lamiaceae reflects its morphological and genetic similarities with other mint family members. As a member of this family, Glechoma fordii exhibits the typical characteristics of the Lamiaceae family, including opposite leaves and a square stem.
uses: Glechoma fordii is a plant species that has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. It is said to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The plant is also believed to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and promote overall well-being. In traditional Chinese medicine, Glechoma fordii is used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of Glechoma fordii for medicinal use.

preparations: Glechoma fordii is used in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is believed to have various health benefits.
